_meta.full_name: Mini-International Personality Item Pool (Mini-IPIP), 20-item short form of the 50-item IPIP Big-Five Factor Markers (Donnellan, Oswald, Baird, & Lucas, 2006)
_meta.license: Public domain. Per the official IPIP site (ipip.ori.org): "The items and scales are in the public domain, which means that one can copy, edit, translate, or use them for any purpose without asking permission and without paying a fee." The Mini-IPIP items are drawn from Goldberg's IPIP and are freely usable for commercial and non-commercial purposes.
_meta.attribution: Items sourced from the International Personality Item Pool (Goldberg, L. R., et al.), https://ipip.ori.org. Scale developed and validated by Donnellan, M. B., Oswald, F. L., Baird, B. M., & Lucas, R. E. (2006). The Mini-IPIP scales: Tiny-yet-effective measures of the Big Five factors of personality. Psychological Assessment, 18(2), 192-203. Verbatim item text and keying taken from the official IPIP Mini-IPIP Scoring Key.
_meta.scoring_notes: 20 items, 4 per Big Five domain. Response scale is a 5-point accuracy scale (1 = Very Inaccurate, 2 = Moderately Inaccurate, 3 = Neither Inaccurate nor Accurate, 4 = Moderately Accurate, 5 = Very Accurate). Domain score = mean (or sum) of its 4 items after reverse-scoring negatively keyed items (reverse: 6 becomes new value = 6 - raw on the 1-5 scale). 11 of 20 items are negatively keyed (items 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 in the administration order below). IPIP-reported internal consistencies (alphas) on the official key: Extraversion .77, Agreeableness .70, Conscientiousness .69, Neuroticism .68, Intellect/Imagination .65. Each IPIP item is completed as a self-description with the implied stem "I ..." (e.g., "[I] Am the life of the party.").
_meta.cautions: All 20 item texts and their +/- keying were fetched verbatim from the primary, public-domain IPIP Mini-IPIP Scoring Key (https://ipip.ori.org/MiniIPIPKey.htm), which groups items by factor and labels each "+ Keyed" or "- Keyed" but does NOT print a 1-20 item number. The numbered administration order (num 1-20) shown here was taken from a secondary source (PsychKit, https://psychkit.org/mini-ipip-big-five-20/); it reproduces the standard Donnellan et al. (2006) presentation order and is fully consistent with the primary source (identical item text, identical factor assignments, identical 11 reverse-keyed items). If exact original ordering is critical, verify against the Donnellan et al. (2006) article appendix. Factor V is labeled "Intellect/Imagination" on the IPIP key (following Goldberg); it corresponds to the Big Five "Openness/Openness to Experience" domain and some sources (including PsychKit) label it "Openness" — same construct. Note: a helper model twice refused to re-transcribe the IPIP key citing "copyright," but this is incorrect — the IPIP is explicitly public domain; the item text used here is from the successful primary fetch. Mini-IPIP measures domain level only and has no facet structure, so facet is left blank for all items.

verification.summary: adversarially fact-checked by a second agent vs primary sources (deepinme wide-collection workflow, 2026-07)

sources.title: Mini-IPIP Scoring Key (official IPIP, primary source for verbatim items and keying)
Mini-IPIP Big Five (20-item) - PsychKit (numbered administration order, corroborating source)
Donnellan, Oswald, Baird & Lucas (2006), The Mini-IPIP scales, Psychological Assessment 18(2):192-203 (PubMed)

items.text: Am the life of the party.
Am not interested in other people's problems.
Often forget to put things back in their proper place.
Am relaxed most of the time.
Talk to a lot of different people at parties.
Am not interested in abstract ideas.
Am not really interested in others.
Do not have a good imagination.
